channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Where the word 'Olympics' comes from and what it means to someone who competed there
item.description: It's a word that evokes national pride and rare talent, and one that has been around for thousands of years.

item.title: Democrats Plan to Counter Trump’s Speech to Congress With Rally
item.description: The House Democratic leader has asked rank-and-file members to sit quietly at the speech or skip it altogether, wary of creating a distraction.

item.title: Surprise four-legged Olympic competitor wows cross-country fans
item.description: Nazgul sprints on course at cross-country ski race, crossing finish line in unsanctioned quest for glory

item.title: Talks on Russia-Ukraine War Enter 2nd Day in Geneva
item.description: The discussions in Geneva were expected to focus on Ukrainian-held territory in the east that Russia wants to control as the price of peace. Kyiv has ...

item.title: Nine Skiers Still Missing After Lake Tahoe Avalanche
item.description: The avalanche struck a guided backcountry skiing group near Truckee, Calif., near the end of a multiday trip, officials said. Six skiers have been res...

item.title: 8 creative ways to build your village, according to our listeners
item.description: NPR listeners share how they've made relationships with their neighbors and community. Many of them, through parties, potlucks and coffees, say they'v...

item.title: State public-option health plans expand but can't fill gaps left by federal changes
item.description: Nevada recently became the third state to offer one of the plans on the ACA marketplaces. They're intended to be a cheaper insurance option but so far...

item.title: An Architect of Virginia’s Redistricting Will Run for a New Seat Himself
item.description: Dan Helmer, a Democratic state lawmaker, played a key role in putting redrawn congressional maps before state voters.

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg to face jury in landmark social media addiction trial
item.description: The case is seen as a test of social media's legal responsibility for platform design features that plaintiffs' lawyers say exacerbated mental health ...

item.title: Botswana’s ‘Corn Lady’ Finds Fame With Food Stands
item.description: Tshephiso Marumo has had success selling traditional village food in Botswana. But as she has risen, she has become more outspoken, inviting backlash.

item.title: A Road Trip from Kandahar to Kabul on Highway 1 in Afghanistan
item.description: Life and business are back along a road once defined by war damage. But even with improved security, Afghans are desperate for jobs and development.

item.title: Alberto Tomba Was an ’80s and ’90s Icon. The Olympics Have Brought Him Back.
item.description: Nicknamed “Tomba la Bomba,” the Italian skier was a global superstar before he drifted from the limelight. Decades later, the Winter Games have given ...

item.title: Talks on Russia-Ukraine War Enter 2nd Day in Geneva
item.description: The discussions in Geneva were expected to focus on Ukrainian-held territory in the east that Russia wants to control as the price of peace. Kyiv has ...

item.title: The A.I. Disruption Has Arrived, and It Sure Is Fun
item.description: We’re entering a new renaissance of software development. We should all be excited, despite the uncertainties that lie ahead.

item.title: Ski Mountaineering Makes Its Olympic Debut at the Milan-Cortina Winter Games
item.description: Ski mountaineering tests strength and endurance as athletes race uphill on skis and on foot before going back down.
